title: did::hex_encode: O(n) String allocations per byte
description:
LOCATION: src/did.rs:120-122

FINDING: hex_encode uses .iter().map(|b| format!("{b:02x}")).collect::<String>(), which allocates one String per byte (64 allocations for a 32-byte key). The idiomatic approach is to pre-allocate a String with capacity 2*len and use write!() or push the two nibble chars directly.

IMPACT: 64 heap allocations per hex_encode call instead of 1. Called during persona creation (persona::add) and DID backfill. Not a hot path, but the current pattern is misleading as a template for future code.
